title: khaya ivorensis
intro: Khaya ivorensis, also known as the Iroko tree, is a species of tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, which contains compounds such as tannins, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Khaya ivorensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Khaya ivorensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in African traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
